name: Kennel Cough (Infectious Tracheobronchitis)
description: Kennel cough is a highly contagious respiratory disease caused by a combination of pathogens including Bordetella bronchiseptica, canine parainfluenza virus, and others. It spreads readily in environments where dogs congregate.
treatment_overview: Most cases self-resolve in 1-3 weeks. Antibiotics (doxycycline) are prescribed for severe cases or dogs at risk of pneumonia. Cough suppressants and rest. Vaccination is effective prevention.
prevention_tips: Regular vaccine boosters for dogs in high-risk environments
